description: Letter to the writer's 'son' ʿAbd al-Wāḥīd. In Judaeo-Arabic. The writer repeats half a dozen times that the addressee should not believe the false rumors that the writer gloats at his misfortunes. On the contrary, his misfortune is the writer's misfortune and his happiness is the writer's happiness. He further writes that the addressee's mother is constantly praying for him.
description_he: מכתב ל'בנו' של הכותב עבד אל-ואחיד. ביהודית-ערבית. הכותב חוזר שוב ושוב שהנמען לא יאמין לשמועות שווא שהכותב שמח לאידו. 'צרתו צרתי ושמחתו שמחתי.' אמו מתפללת עבורו תמיד.
